name: azure-communication-callautomation-java
description: Build call automation workflows with Azure Communication Services Call Automation Java SDK. Use when implementing IVR systems, call routing, call recording, DTMF recognition, text-to-speech, or AI-powered call flows.
capabilities: Create and manage outbound and inbound calls with CallAutomationClient
Implement IVR systems with DTMF tone recognition and text-to-speech prompts
Handle call recording including start, pause, resume, stop, and download
Process call events via webhooks using CallAutomationEventParser
Add participants, transfer calls, and manage call lifecycle
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Evaluated 44 static findings. All flagged issues are false positives. The file contains legitimate Azure SDK documentation with Java code examples. External commands detection was triggered by Java code block syntax. Network and URL detections are placeholder documentation patterns. No malicious code or security risks present.
valueStatement: This skill provides code examples and documentation for Azure Communication Services Call Automation Java SDK. It enables developers to build IVR systems, handle incoming calls, manage call recordings, and implement speech recognition.
userTitle: Build Azure Call Automation with Java
limitations: Requires Azure Communication Services subscription and configured endpoints
Skill provides documentation and code examples only, not runtime execution
Does not include Azure resource provisioning or infrastructure setup

useCases.targetUser: Java developers building customer service phone systems
useCases.title: Build IVR Call Flows
useCases.description: Create interactive voice response systems that greet callers, collect DTMF input, and route calls based on user selections.

useCases.targetUser: Enterprise developers needing call audit trails
useCases.title: Implement Call Recording
useCases.description: Add call recording capabilities to compliance, quality assurance, or analytics workflows.

useCases.targetUser: Backend developers integrating telephony with applications
useCases.title: Handle Incoming Calls
useCases.description: Answer and process incoming calls with webhook-based event handling for automated call routing.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Outbound Call
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to create an outbound call to a phone number using Azure Communication Services Call Automation Java SDK. Include client setup with connection string.
promptTemplates.scenario: Setting up a simple outbound call

promptTemplates.title: IVR with DTMF
promptTemplates.prompt: Write Java code for an IVR system that plays a welcome message, collects DTMF input with timeout handling, and processes the result.

promptTemplates.title: Call Recording Flow
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I start a call recording, handle the RecordingFileStatusUpdated event, and download the recording file?

promptTemplates.title: Event Handling
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a webhook handler in Java that processes CallConnected, RecognizeCompleted, and CallDisconnected events from Azure Communication Services.

bestPractices: Use DefaultAzureCredential for secure authentication in production environments
Implement proper webhook signature validation to verify event authenticity
Handle all CallAutomationEventBase types in your event processor for robust error handling
antiPatterns: Do not hardcode connection strings in source code - use environment variables or Azure Key Vault
Avoid long-running recognize operations without proper timeout configuration
Do not ignore error responses from API calls - always implement proper exception handling

faq.question: What is Azure Communication Services Call Automation?
faq.answer: Azure Communication Services Call Automation is a cloud-based API that enables developers to build server-side call control workflows including IVR systems, call recording, and AI-powered voice interactions.

faq.question: How do I authenticate with Azure Communication Services?
faq.answer: You can authenticate using either a connection string or Azure Identity. For production, use DefaultAzureCredential which supports managed identities and service principals.

faq.question: Can I record calls with this SDK?
faq.answer: Yes, the SDK supports call recording with options for mixed or single-channel audio, various formats including MP4, and controls for pause, resume, and stop.

faq.question: How do I handle incoming calls?
faq.answer: Subscribe to Event Grid for IncomingCall events, then use the AnswerCallOptions with the incoming call context to answer the call programmatically.

faq.question: What is DTMF and how do I recognize it?
faq.answer: DTMF (Dual-Tone Multi-Frequency) is the tones produced by phone keypad presses. Use CallMediaRecognizeDtmfOptions to collect tone input with configurable timeouts.

faq.question: Does this work with Microsoft Teams calls?
faq.answer: Yes, Azure Communication Services can interoperate with Microsoft Teams, allowing you to add participants from Teams to ACS calls or vice versa.
